subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Infrastructure and Resilient Design
description: A quick win: understand Infrastructure and Resilient Design.

hook.text: Tokyo's skyscrapers sit on giant rubber pads that absorb earthquake energy. Buildings bounce, not break.

buildup.text: Resilient design builds infrastructure that bends and absorbs energy instead of shattering.

discovery.text: Levees, sea walls, and flood channels protect millions from water-based disasters.

twist.text: Over-reliance on infrastructure creates complacency—New Orleans' levees failed in Katrina.

climax.text: The best resilience combines strong infrastructure with educated, prepared populations.

punchline.text: Build strong walls. But always know what to do when they fail.

quiz.question: What is a risk of relying too heavily on disaster infrastructure like levees?
options: It creates complacency and reduces personal preparedness
